metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1869
metadata.comment:
Rationale: mTOR siRNA transfection reduced mTOR (Fig. 8I) and phospho-p70S6K (Fig. 8J) in dendrites upon BDNF treatment in cortical cultures. Figure 8L shows the intensity of red fluorescence, which reflects phospho-S6K signal, in each dendrite that is measured.

In Figure 9 the authors analysed phosph-4EBP levels in isolated dendrites. Even in isolated dendrites, BDNF increased and rapamycin decreased the levels of phosphorylated 4EBP (Fig. 9A-F), indicating the local activation of mTOR-dependent translation in dendrites.

14/11/2017 Pim
- The BDNF-induced activation of protein translation is rapamycin-sensitive. Rapamycin is an antagonist of mTOR.
- The phospho state of 4EBP1 is used as a readout of the efffect of mTOR on protein translation.\
- Literal: "BDNF increased protein synthesis in the synaptoneurosomes, a concentrated fraction of presynaptic and postsynaptic components prepared from rat brain" --> shows synaptic context. Experimental description: Cortical neuronal cultured prepared at E17 and used for experiments at DIV10-14
(From Takei et al., 1998)

14/11/2017 Pim
- Species of the neuronal culture not stated in the paper. Species traced back to rat via PMID:15525761 referenced in the M&M section: "Briefly, cerebral cortices were removed from 17-day-old embryonic rat fetuses and dissociated using trypsin/DNase I."
